back_translation: 'You, bring near to you Aaron your brother and his sons with him, from among the sons of Israel, to serve me as priests — Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, sons of Aaron.'
'Make holy garments for Aaron your brother, for glory and for splendor.'
'You shall speak to all the wise of heart, whom I have filled with a spirit of wisdom, and they shall make Aaron's garments to consecrate him to serve me as priest.'

'These are the garments that they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a woven tunic, a turban, and a sash. They shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother and his sons, to serve me as priests.'
'They shall take the gold, the blue, the purple, the scarlet, and the fine linen.'
'They shall make the ephod of gold, blue and purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen — the work of a skilled craftsman.'
'It shall have two joining shoulder pieces at its two ends, and shall be joined.'
'The skillfully woven waistband of the ephod that is on it shall be of the same work, of one piece with it — gold, blue and purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine twisted linen.'
'You shall take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel:'
'Six of their names on one stone, and the names of the remaining six on the second stone, in the order of their birth.'
'The work of a stone engraver — like seal engravings — you shall engrave on the two stones with the names of the sons of Israel. Mount them in filigree settings of gold.'
'You shall set the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod, as memorial stones for the sons of Israel. Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD on his two shoulders as a memorial.'
'You shall make filigree settings of gold,'
'and two chains of pure gold — twisted like cord work — you shall make them, and attach the corded chains to the settings.'
'You shall make a breastpiece of judgment, the work of a skilled craftsman; like the work of the ephod you shall make it — of gold, blue and purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine twisted linen.'
'It shall be square when doubled — a span long and a span wide.'
'You shall set in it a setting of stones — four rows of stones: a row of ruby, topaz, and emerald — the first row.'
'And the second row: turquoise, sapphire, and diamond.'
'And the third row: jacinth, agate, and amethyst.'
'And the fourth row: beryl, onyx, and jasper. They shall be mounted in gold in their settings.'
'The stones shall be twelve, corresponding to the names of the sons of Israel; like seal engravings, each with his name — for the twelve tribes.'
'You shall make on the breastpiece twisted chains like cord work — of pure gold.'
'You shall make on the breastpiece two rings of gold, and put the two rings on the two corners of the breastpiece.'
'Put the two gold cords on the two rings at the corners of the breastpiece.'
'The other two ends of the two cords you shall put on the two filigree settings, attaching them to the shoulder pieces of the ephod on its front.'
'You shall make two rings of gold and place them on the two corners of the breastpiece, on its edge that is toward the inside of the ephod.'
'You shall make two rings of gold and put them on the two shoulder pieces of the ephod below, at the front of it, close to its seam, above the woven waistband of the ephod.'
'They shall tie the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a cord of blue, so that it is over the waistband of the ephod and the breastpiece does not come loose from the ephod.'
'Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment over his heart, when he enters the Holy Place — for a memorial before the LORD continually.'

'You shall put in the breastpiece of judgment the Urim and the Thummim, and they shall be over Aaron's heart when he goes in before the LORD; Aaron shall continually bear the judgment of the sons of Israel over his heart before the LORD.'
'You shall make the robe of the ephod entirely of blue.'
'There shall be an opening at its top in its midst; a binding shall be at its opening round about, the work of a weaver, like the opening of a coat of mail it shall be for it — it shall not be torn.'
'You shall make on its hem pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet, on its hem round about; and gold bells between them round about.'
'A gold bell and a pomegranate, a gold bell and a pomegranate, on the hem of the robe round about.'
'Aaron shall wear it to minister; its sound shall be heard when he enters the Holy Place before the LORD and when he comes out, so that he does not die.'
'You shall make a plate of pure gold, and engrave on it like seal engravings: HOLY TO THE LORD.'
'You shall put it on a cord of blue, and it shall be on the turban; it shall be on the front of the turban.'

'It shall be on Aaron's forehead, and Aaron shall bear the iniquity of the holy things that the sons of Israel consecrate for all their holy gifts; and it shall be on his forehead continually for acceptance for them before the LORD.'
'You shall weave the tunic of fine linen, and make the turban of fine linen; the sash you shall make the work of an embroiderer.'
'For Aaron's sons you shall make tunics, and you shall make for them sashes, and headbands you shall make for them — for glory and for splendor.'
'You shall clothe with them Aaron your brother and his sons with him; you shall anoint them, ordain them, and consecrate them, and they shall serve as priests to me.'
'Make for them linen undergarments to cover the flesh of their nakedness; from the waist to the thighs they shall be.'

'They shall be on Aaron and on his sons when they enter the Tent of Meeting or when they approach the altar to minister in the Holy Place, so that they do not bear guilt and die. This is a perpetual statute for him and his descendants after him.'
